{"id":"bd-sve.2","title":"Build MemoryStore — core markdown-based read/write/search","description":"Core memory abstraction that stores entries as individual markdown files with YAML frontmatter. Serves both agent memory (.village/memory/) and Elder KB (wiki/).

**File:** village/memory.py

**MemoryStore class:**
- __init__(store_path: Path) — e.g. .village/memory/ or wiki/
- put(title, text, tags=[], metadata={}, source=None) -> str — write new entry, return ID
- get(entry_id: str) -> MemoryEntry | None — retrieve single entry
- find(query: str, k: int = 5) -> list[MemoryEntry] — keyword search (case-insensitive substring over title+text+tags)
- recent(limit: int = 10) -> list[MemoryEntry] — most recent by created timestamp
- related(entry_id: str, k: int = 5) -> list[MemoryEntry] — entries with tag overlap
- all_entries() -> list[MemoryEntry] — list all entries
- delete(entry_id: str) -> bool — remove entry
- rebuild_index() -> None — regenerate index.md from entries

**MemoryEntry dataclass:**
- id: str (auto-generated, e.g. note-001)
- title: str
- text: str
- tags: list[str]
- created: datetime
- metadata: dict (session, source, related entries, etc.)

**Entry file format (markdown + YAML frontmatter):**
```markdown
---
id: note-001
tags: [auth, configuration, gotcha]
created: 2026-04-08T10:30:00
session: abc123
source: https://docs.example.com/auth
related: [note-003, note-007]
---

# Title here

Entry content goes here.
```

**index.md format:**
```markdown
# Memory Index

- [note-001] Title here — auth, configuration, gotcha
- [note-002] Another entry — tags, here
```

**Storage layout:**
```
.village/memory/
├── index.md
└── entries/
    ├── note-001.md
    └── note-002.md
```

**No external dependencies** — uses stdlib only (pathlib, yaml via custom parser or stdlib, datetime, re).

**Tests:** tests/test_memory.py
- Test put/get roundtrip
- Test find by keyword
- Test recent ordering
- Test related by tag overlap
- Test rebuild_index
- Test delete
- Test empty store behavior","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"github@bkuri.com","created_at":"2026-04-08T21:25:45Z","created_by":"Bernardo Kuri","updated_at":"2026-04-08T21:25:45Z"}

new_value: Built MemoryStore in village/memory.py with put/get/find/recent/related/delete/rebuild_index. 23 tests passing.

{"id":"bd-jv0.2","title":"Build cross-linking engine","description":"Find related wiki pages and maintain bidirectional cross-references. Called by ElderStore.see() after each ingestion.

**File:** village/elder/crosslink.py

**Functions:**
- find_related(store: MemoryStore, entry: MemoryEntry, k: int = 5) -> list[tuple[MemoryEntry, float]]
  — Score entries by tag overlap and text similarity. Returns (entry, score) tuples.
  — Scoring: tag_jaccard * 0.6 + title_keyword_overlap * 0.4
  — Filter out score < 0.2

- update_cross_references(store: MemoryStore, source_id: str, related_ids: list[str]) -> int
  — Add source_id to related field of each related entry
  — Add related_ids to related field of source entry
  — Returns count of pages updated

**Cross-reference format in frontmatter:**
```yaml
related: [auth-setup, configuration, worktree-lifecycle]
```

**Wiki-link format in page body:**
```markdown
## Related
- [[auth-setup]] — Environment variables for auth setup
- [[configuration]] — Project configuration overview
```

**Tests:** tests/test_crosslink.py
- Test find_related with tag overlap
- Test find_related with no related entries
- Test update_cross_references bidirectional
- Test scoring thresholds","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"github@bkuri.com","created_at":"2026-04-08T21:26:33Z","created_by":"Bernardo Kuri","updated_at":"2026-04-08T21:26:33Z"}

{"id":"bd-jv0.3","title":"Build curation engine (curate/upkeep)","description":"Health checks and maintenance for the wiki. Finds stale content, orphans, contradictions, and missing cross-references.

**File:** village/elder/curate.py

**CurateResult dataclass:**
- broken_links: list[BrokenLink] — URLs that return 404
- orphans: list[str] — page IDs with no inbound references
- stale_entries: list[StaleEntry] — entries older than threshold with no recent verification
- contradictions: list[Contradiction] — conflicting claims between pages (LLM-powered)
- missing_links: list[MissingLink] — pages that should reference each other but don't
- voice_updated: bool — whether VOICE.md was regenerated

**Curator class:**
- __init__(store: ElderStore, config: CurateConfig)
- check_links() -> list[BrokenLink] — HTTP HEAD for URL sources
- find_orphans() -> list[str] — entries with no inbound related refs
- find_stale(max_age_days: int = 90) -> list[StaleEntry]
- find_contradictions() -> list[Contradiction] — LLM analyzes pairs of related pages
- suggest_links() -> list[MissingLink] — pages with tag overlap but no cross-ref
- curate() -> CurateResult — run all checks + regenerate VOICE.md

**VOICE.md generation:**
After curate runs, regenerate VOICE.md at project root:
```markdown
# Village Voice — <project name>
> Last updated: 2026-04-08 by Village Elder

## Summary
<LLM-distilled 1-2 paragraph overview>

## Recent Activity
- [date] Brief description (see: page-id)

## Conventions
- Bulleted list of project conventions from wiki

## Known Gotchas
- Bulleted list of gotchas/mistakes from wiki

## Active Questions
- Unresolved questions from wiki

## Wiki Index
- [page-id] — One-line summary
```

**CurateConfig:**
- stale_threshold_days: int = 90
- check_urls: bool = True
- llm_analyze: bool = True (for contradictions)

**Tests:** tests/test_curate.py
- Test find_orphans with known orphan
- Test find_orphans with fully connected wiki
- Test suggest_links finds missing connections
- Test VOICE.md generation
- Test check_links with mock HTTP","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"github@bkuri.com","created_at":"2026-04-08T21:26:54Z","created_by":"Bernardo Kuri","updated_at":"2026-04-08T21:26:54Z"}

{"id":"bd-jv0.1","title":"Build ElderStore — ingestion pipeline and content extraction","description":"Build the Elder knowledge base store extending MemoryStore with ingestion capabilities.

**File:** village/elder.py

**ElderStore(MemoryStore):**
- __init__(wiki_path: Path) — path to wiki/ directory
- see(source: str | Path) -> IngestResult — ingest URL or file, create summary, index, cross-link
- ask(question: str) -> AskResult — query wiki, synthesize answer (future task, stub for now)

**IngestResult dataclass:**
- entry_id: str
- title: str
- related_count: int (number of pages cross-linked)
- status: str (success, error)

**Wiki directory structure:**
```
wiki/
├── ingest/        # Drop sources here (monitored)
├── processed/     # Moved after successful ingestion
├── pages/         # LLM-generated wiki pages (inherits MemoryStore entries/)
│   ├── auth-setup.md
│   └── configuration.md
├── index.md       # Auto-generated catalog
└── log.md         # Chronological record
```

**Ingestion pipeline (see method):**
1. Detect source type (URL vs file)
2. Extract content:
   - URL: fetch via httpx, convert HTML→markdown (trafilatura)
   - .md/.txt: read directly
   - .pdf: extract text (stdlib or minimal lib)
3. Generate summary via LLM (reuse village/llm/ module)
4. Create wiki page in pages/ with YAML frontmatter
5. Append to log.md with timestamp and source
6. Update index.md
7. Trigger cross-linking (delegates to crosslink module, bd-jv0.2)

**Dependency:** Add trafilatura to pyproject.toml

**Tests:** tests/test_elder.py
- Test see with local markdown file
- Test see with text file
- Test see with invalid source
- Test log.md appended correctly
- Test index.md updated correctly
- Test processed file moved to processed/","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"github@bkuri.com","created_at":"2026-04-08T21:26:19Z","created_by":"Bernardo Kuri","updated_at":"2026-04-08T21:26:19Z"}

**Commands:**

```bash
village elder see <url|file> [--tags tag1,tag2]   # Ingest + cross-link
village elder fetch <url|file> [--tags tag1,tag2]  # Alias for see
village elder ask <question> [--save]              # Query + synthesize
village elder curate                               # Health check + repair + regenerate VOICE.md
village elder upkeep                               # Alias for curate
village elder stats                                # Show wiki stats (page count, recent activity, orphans)

```

**Register in village/cli/__init__.py:**
```python
from village.cli import elder
village.add_command(elder.elder_group)
```

- ask: Call ElderStore.ask(), print answer and sources

- stats: Print page count, recent entries, orphan count, last curated date

{"id":"bd-jv0.6","title":"Build monitor — polling-based file watcher for wiki/ingest/","description":"Simple polling-based file watcher for wiki/ingest/ directory. No additional dependencies (no watchdog).

**File:** village/elder/monitor.py

**Monitor class:**
- __init__(wiki_path: Path, store: ElderStore, poll_interval: int = 30)
- start() -> None — blocking loop, poll ingest/ directory
- stop() -> None — signal to stop polling
- poll() -> list[IngestResult] — single pass: list new files, ingest each

**Implementation:**
- Track seen files in .village/monitor_state.json (just a set of filenames)
- Each poll: os.listdir(wiki/ingest/) - seen_files = new_files
- For each new file: call store.see(file)
- Graceful shutdown on SIGINT/SIGTERM
- Configurable poll_interval via [memory] config or CLI --interval flag

**No dependencies** — uses os.listdir, time.sleep, signal, json

**Tests:** tests/test_monitor.py
- Test poll with new file
- Test poll with no new files
- Test poll with multiple new files
- Test seen state persistence
- Test graceful stop","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"github@bkuri.com","created_at":"2026-04-08T21:27:37Z","created_by":"Bernardo Kuri","updated_at":"2026-04-08T21:27:37Z"}

{"id":"bd-jv0.4","title":"Build ask command — query wiki and synthesize answers","description":"Implement the ask method on ElderStore. Queries the wiki using keyword search, reads relevant pages, and synthesizes an answer via LLM.

**File:** village/elder.py (add to existing ElderStore class)

**AskResult dataclass:**
- answer: str — synthesized answer text
- sources: list[str] — page IDs used as source material
- saved: bool — whether the answer was saved as a new page

**ElderStore.ask(question: str, save: bool = False) -> AskResult:**
1. store.find(question, k=5) — keyword search for relevant pages
2. Read full text of top-k matching pages
3. Call LLM with: question + page contents as context
4. LLM synthesizes answer with citations ([page-id])
5. If save=True, create new wiki page from the answer
6. Return AskResult

**LLM integration:**
- Reuse village/llm/ module (same client as chat)
- System prompt: 'You are a knowledge base assistant. Answer based on the provided wiki pages. Cite sources as [page-id]. If unsure, say so.'
- Configurable model via [memory] config or fallback to default

**Tests:** tests/test_elder.py (add to existing)
- Test ask with matching pages
- Test ask with no matching pages
- Test ask with save=True creates new page
- Test citations in answer","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"github@bkuri.com","created_at":"2026-04-08T21:27:08Z","created_by":"Bernardo Kuri","updated_at":"2026-04-08T21:27:08Z"}

## PPC Integration & Document Grounding (from presentation)

When adding LLM synthesis, use PPC profile `village-elder` with:

**Grounding prompt (3-rule pattern from doc extraction presentation):**
1. Base answer ONLY on the wiki pages provided. Nothing else.
2. If information isn't found, say 'Not found in wiki.' Don't guess.
3. For each claim, cite [page-id] and include a relevant quote.

**Additional features:**
- [Unverified] tagging for uncertain matches (middle ground between found/not-found)
- Self-check verification as optional --verify flag: re-scan wiki to verify each claim has supporting quote
- Multi-model cross-validation (future enhancement)
- PPC profile: profiles/village-elder.yml (mode: elder-query, traits: grounded,conservative)
- Contract: prompts/contracts/elder-answer.md (structured answer with citations)"}

new_value:
{"notes":"

## BRUTAL Method Integration (from presentation)

The interview system prompt must incorporate the B.R.U.T.A.L. method:

**B (Begin Fresh):** Each interview runs in a clean LLM context with no session memory leaking in.

**R (Right Model):** Uses openrouter/auto as default (already in config). Override via [onboard].interview_model.

**U (Critic Persona):** System prompt should NOT be a helpful assistant. Use one of three critic personas:
- devil's-advocate: basic counterarguments, daily driver
- red-team: actively hunt for weaknesses (default)
- gordon-ramsay: extremely critical, harsh, but actionable

**T (Third-Party Framing):** Frame questions about a colleague's project, not the user's. 'A colleague described their project as [X]. What questions would you need answered?' This makes the LLM more probing (it protects the user's ego less).

**A (Ask Specific Questions):** Pointed questions, not vague. Examples:
- 'What's the weakest part of this architecture that's easy to miss?'
- 'If this project fails in 6 months, what's the most likely reason?'
- 'What would a skeptical senior engineer say is the biggest risk?'

**L (Self-Critique):** Not here — applied in the generator phase (bd-73m.5).

## PPC Integration
- Interview prompt composed via PPC profile (profiles/village-onboard.yml)
- Mode: onboard, traits: critical/probing, contract: onboard-result
- Prompt module: prompts/modes/onboard-interview.md
- This replaces hardcoded system prompts with PPC-composed prompts"}

{"id":"bd-73m.4","title":"Build adaptive interview engine","description":"LLM-powered adaptive interview using the reverse-prompting pattern. One question at a time, uses previous answers to inform next question.

**File:** village/onboard/interview.py

**InterviewResult dataclass:**
- answers: dict[str, str] (question -> answer)
- project_summary: str
- raw_transcript: list[tuple[str, str]] (question, answer pairs)

**InterviewEngine class:**
- __init__(config: OnboardConfig, project_info: ProjectInfo, scaffold: ScaffoldTemplate)
- run() -> InterviewResult — main interview loop

**System prompt (the four layers):**
```
Role: You are an expert software project architect who specializes in onboarding 
developers and AI agents to new codebases.

Goal: Extract enough project context to generate complete AGENTS.md, README.md, 
and initial wiki pages. Focus on what makes this project unique.

Mechanic: Ask me one question at a time. Use my previous response to inform your 
next question. If I seem confused or uncertain, guide me using context from my 
previous answers.

Guardrails: Ask a maximum of {max_questions} questions. End with 'INTERVIEW_COMPLETE' 
when you have enough information or have reached the limit.
```

**Context seeding:**
The first message to the LLM includes detected project info:
```
I'm setting up a {language} project called {name}.
Detected: {build_tool}, {test_runner}, {linter}, {framework}
I need you to interview me about this project to create complete documentation.
Start with the most important question.
```

**Interview loop:**
1. Send seeded context to LLM
2. LLM asks first question
3. Display question, collect user input (multiline: Enter submits, Shift+Enter for newline)
4. Send answer back to LLM
5. LLM asks next question (or says INTERVIEW_COMPLETE)
6. Repeat up to max_questions
7. Return InterviewResult

**Skip handling:**
- Empty answer → skip question, LLM moves to next
- 'done' → end interview early
- 'skip' → skip this question

**Input handling:**
- Use click.prompt() or sys.stdin for multiline input
- Support Shift+Enter / Ctrl+J for newlines
- Enter on empty line submits

**Tests:** tests/test_onboard_interview.py
- Test interview with mocked LLM responses
- Test max_questions limit respected
- Test 'done' ends interview early
- Test skip handling
- Test context seeding includes project info
- Test InterviewResult structure","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"github@bkuri.com","created_at":"2026-04-09T00:05:34Z","created_by":"Bernardo Kuri","updated_at":"2026-04-09T00:05:34Z"}

{"id":"bd-73m.5","title":"Build file generator — AGENTS.md, README.md, wiki seeds","description":"Generate complete project files from interview results. Uses LLM to synthesize interview answers + scaffold template into polished output files.

**GenerationResult dataclass:**

**Generator class:**
- __init__(project_info: ProjectInfo, scaffold: ScaffoldTemplate, interview: InterviewResult, project_root: Path)
- generate() -> GenerationResult
- write_files(result: GenerationResult) -> list[str] — write files to disk, return list of created paths

**AGENTS.md generation (LLM-powered):**
System prompt: 'Generate a complete AGENTS.md for a {language} project based on the interview answers. Include all sections: build commands, lint, test, code style, project structure, integration points, constraints. Be specific, not generic.'

Input: interview answers + scaffold template (build commands, conventions, etc.)
Output: Complete AGENTS.md with real commands and conventions.

**README.md generation (LLM-powered):**
System prompt: 'Generate a complete README.md based on the interview. Include project description, getting started, architecture overview, usage examples.'

Input: interview answers + project info
Output: Complete README.md.

**Wiki seeds (LLM-powered):**
System prompt: 'Based on the interview, create 2-3 wiki pages for the project knowledge base. Each page should cover a key aspect of the project.'

Output files dropped into wiki/ingest/:
- project-overview.md
- conventions.md
- (optional: architecture.md or other relevant pages)

**Important:** VOICE.md is NOT generated here. Elder handles that via curate after processing wiki seeds.

**Tests:** tests/test_onboard_generator.py
- Test generate produces all required fields
- Test write_files creates files on disk

- Test AGENTS.md doesn't contain placeholder text

{"notes":"

## PPC Integration & Document Grounding (from presentation)

When adding LLM synthesis, use PPC profile `village-elder` with:

**Grounding prompt (3-rule pattern from doc extraction presentation):**
1. Base answer ONLY on the wiki pages provided. Nothing else.
2. If information isn't found, say 'Not found in wiki.' Don't guess.
3. For each claim, cite [page-id] and include a relevant quote.

**Additional features:**
- [Unverified] tagging for uncertain matches (middle ground between found/not-found)
- Self-check verification as optional --verify flag: re-scan wiki to verify each claim has supporting quote
- Multi-model cross-validation (future enhancement)
- PPC profile: profiles/village-elder.yml (mode: elder-query, traits: grounded,conservative)
- Contract: prompts/contracts/elder-answer.md (structured answer with citations)


## Dependencies
- bd-73m.11: PPC profiles and prompt modules for Elder (village-elder.yml, elder-grounding.md, elder-answer.md)"}

{"id":"bd-jv0.4","title":"Build ask command — query wiki and synthesize answers","description":"Implement the ask method on ElderStore. Queries the wiki using keyword search, reads relevant pages, and synthesizes an answer via LLM.

**File:** village/elder.py (add to existing ElderStore class)

**AskResult dataclass:**
- answer: str — synthesized answer text
- sources: list[str] — page IDs used as source material
- saved: bool — whether the answer was saved as a new page

**ElderStore.ask(question: str, save: bool = False) -> AskResult:**
1. store.find(question, k=5) — keyword search for relevant pages
2. Read full text of top-k matching pages
3. Call LLM with: question + page contents as context
4. LLM synthesizes answer with citations ([page-id])
5. If save=True, create new wiki page from the answer
6. Return AskResult

**LLM integration:**
- Reuse village/llm/ module (same client as chat)
- System prompt: 'You are a knowledge base assistant. Answer based on the provided wiki pages. Cite sources as [page-id]. If unsure, say so.'
- Configurable model via [memory] config or fallback to default

**Tests:** tests/test_elder.py (add to existing)
- Test ask with matching pages
- Test ask with no matching pages
- Test ask with save=True creates new page
- Test citations in answer","notes":"

{"id":"bd-73m.1","title":"Build project type detector","description":"Rule-based project detection that scans directory contents to identify language, framework, build tools, and test runners.

**File:** village/onboard/__init__.py, village/onboard/detector.py

**ProjectInfo dataclass:**
- language: str (python, javascript, typescript, rust, go, ruby, java, unknown)
- framework: str | None (fastapi, flask, django, react, nextjs, actix, etc.)
- build_tool: str | None (uv, pip, poetry, npm, yarn, pnpm, cargo, go, bundle, maven, gradle)
- test_runner: str | None (pytest, jest, vitest, cargo test, go test, rspec, junit)
- linter: str | None (ruff, eslint, clippy, golangci-lint, rubocop)
- package_file: Path | None (pyproject.toml, package.json, Cargo.toml, go.mod, Gemfile, pom.xml, build.gradle)
- entry_point: str | None (inferred from package file or directory structure)
- has_existing_agents_md: bool
- has_existing_readme: bool
- needs_onboarding: bool (True if AGENTS.md is template or missing)

**detect_project(path: Path) -> ProjectInfo:**
Scans for:
- pyproject.toml → Python, check [tool.poetry]/[project] for framework deps
- setup.py/setup.cfg → Python (legacy)
- package.json → JS/TS, check dependencies for framework
- Cargo.toml → Rust
- go.mod → Go
- Gemfile → Ruby
- pom.xml/build.gradle → Java
- Makefile/Justfile → generic build
- Also checks for AGENTS.md existence and template content (contains '<fill in>')

**Language-specific detectors:**
- _detect_python(path) -> ProjectInfo
- _detect_javascript(path) -> ProjectInfo
- _detect_rust(path) -> ProjectInfo
- _detect_go(path) -> ProjectInfo

**No LLM calls** — purely rule-based, zero cost.

**Tests:** tests/test_onboard_detector.py
- Test detect_project with pyproject.toml (Python/uv)
- Test detect_project with package.json (JS/React)
- Test detect_project with Cargo.toml (Rust)
- Test detect_project with no package file (unknown)
- Test needs_onboarding detects template AGENTS.md
- Test needs_onboarding detects missing AGENTS.md
- Test needs_onboarding False with complete AGENTS.md","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"github@bkuri.com","created_at":"2026-04-09T00:03:58Z","created_by":"Bernardo Kuri","updated_at":"2026-04-09T00:03:58Z"}

{"id":"bd-73m.11","title":"Create PPC profiles and prompt modules for Elder and Onboarding","description":"Create PPC (Prompt Policy Compiler) profiles and prompt modules for the Elder query system and Onboarding interview.

**New files:**

**Profiles:**
1. profiles/village-elder.yml — Elder query profile
   - mode: elder-query
   - requires: [base, elder-grounding, constraint-check]
   - contract: elder-answer
   - vars: enforce_grounding=true, cite_sources=true, allow_unverified=true

2. profiles/village-onboard.yml — Onboarding interview profile
   - mode: onboard
   - requires: [base, onboard-interview, qa-flow, constraint-check]
   - contract: onboard-result
   - vars: enforce_grounding=true, max_questions=15, self_critique=true, critic_persona=red-team

**Prompt Modules:**
3. prompts/modes/elder-grounding.md — 3-rule grounding prompt (from doc extraction presentation)
   - Rule 1: Base answer ONLY on wiki pages. Nothing else.
   - Rule 2: If not found, say 'Not found in wiki.' Don't guess.
   - Rule 3: For each claim, cite [page-id] + relevant quote.
   - Rule 4: If uncertain, mark as [Unverified].
   - High-stakes mode: 'Only respond with information you are 100% confident is from the wiki.'

4. prompts/modes/onboard-interview.md — BRUTAL-inspired interview mechanics
   - Critic persona selection (devil's-advocate / red-team / gordon-ramsay)
   - Third-party framing: 'A colleague described their project...'
   - Specific question patterns (weakest parts, failure modes, skeptical reviewer)
   - Self-critique instructions for post-generation validation
   - Begin fresh: no session memory reference

**Contracts:**
5. prompts/contracts/elder-answer.md — Structured Elder answer schema
   - Fields: answer, sources (list of {page_id, quote}), confidence (high/unverified/not-found)
   - Citation format: [page-id] inline references
   - Output format: markdown or JSON

6. prompts/contracts/onboard-result.md — Structured onboarding output schema
   - Fields: agents_md, readme_md, wiki_seeds (list of {filename, content})
   - Critique: self_critique_score, rewritten_sections
   - Output format: JSON

**Tests:**
- Verify each profile loads and composes correctly via PPC
- Verify prompt modules contain required sections
- Verify contracts produce valid JSON schemas","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"github@bkuri.com","created_at":"2026-04-09T00:18:13Z","created_by":"Bernardo Kuri","updated_at":"2026-04-09T00:18:13Z"}

{"id":"bd-73m.4","title":"Build adaptive interview engine","description":"LLM-powered adaptive interview using the reverse-prompting pattern. One question at a time, uses previous answers to inform next question.

**File:** village/onboard/interview.py

**InterviewResult dataclass:**
- answers: dict[str, str] (question -> answer)
- project_summary: str
- raw_transcript: list[tuple[str, str]] (question, answer pairs)

**InterviewEngine class:**
- __init__(config: OnboardConfig, project_info: ProjectInfo, scaffold: ScaffoldTemplate)
- run() -> InterviewResult — main interview loop

**System prompt (the four layers):**
```
Role: You are an expert software project architect who specializes in onboarding 
developers and AI agents to new codebases.

Goal: Extract enough project context to generate complete AGENTS.md, README.md, 
and initial wiki pages. Focus on what makes this project unique.

Mechanic: Ask me one question at a time. Use my previous response to inform your 
next question. If I seem confused or uncertain, guide me using context from my 
previous answers.

Guardrails: Ask a maximum of {max_questions} questions. End with 'INTERVIEW_COMPLETE' 
when you have enough information or have reached the limit.
```

**Context seeding:**
The first message to the LLM includes detected project info:
```
I'm setting up a {language} project called {name}.
Detected: {build_tool}, {test_runner}, {linter}, {framework}
I need you to interview me about this project to create complete documentation.
Start with the most important question.
```

**Interview loop:**
1. Send seeded context to LLM
2. LLM asks first question
3. Display question, collect user input (multiline: Enter submits, Shift+Enter for newline)
4. Send answer back to LLM
5. LLM asks next question (or says INTERVIEW_COMPLETE)
6. Repeat up to max_questions
7. Return InterviewResult

**Skip handling:**
- Empty answer → skip question, LLM moves to next
- 'done' → end interview early
- 'skip' → skip this question

**Input handling:**
- Use click.prompt() or sys.stdin for multiline input
- Support Shift+Enter / Ctrl+J for newlines
- Enter on empty line submits

**Tests:** tests/test_onboard_interview.py
- Test interview with mocked LLM responses
- Test max_questions limit respected
- Test 'done' ends interview early
- Test skip handling
- Test context seeding includes project info
- Test InterviewResult structure","notes":"

## BRUTAL Method Integration (from presentation)

The interview system prompt must incorporate the B.R.U.T.A.L. method:

**B (Begin Fresh):** Each interview runs in a clean LLM context with no session memory leaking in.

**R (Right Model):** Uses openrouter/auto as default (already in config). Override via [onboard].interview_model.

**U (Critic Persona):** System prompt should NOT be a helpful assistant. Use one of three critic personas:
- devil's-advocate: basic counterarguments, daily driver
- red-team: actively hunt for weaknesses (default)
- gordon-ramsay: extremely critical, harsh, but actionable

**T (Third-Party Framing):** Frame questions about a colleague's project, not the user's. 'A colleague described their project as [X]. What questions would you need answered?' This makes the LLM more probing (it protects the user's ego less).

**A (Ask Specific Questions):** Pointed questions, not vague. Examples:
- 'What's the weakest part of this architecture that's easy to miss?'
- 'If this project fails in 6 months, what's the most likely reason?'
- 'What would a skeptical senior engineer say is the biggest risk?'

**L (Self-Critique):** Not here — applied in the generator phase (bd-73m.5).

## PPC Integration
- Interview prompt composed via PPC profile (profiles/village-onboard.yml)
- Mode: onboard, traits: critical/probing, contract: onboard-result
- Prompt module: prompts/modes/onboard-interview.md
- This replaces hardcoded system prompts with PPC-composed prompts","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"github@bkuri.com","created_at":"2026-04-09T00:05:34Z","created_by":"Bernardo Kuri","updated_at":"2026-04-09T00:17:44Z"}

{"id":"bd-73m.7","title":"Wire onboard into village up and add village onboard command","description":"Wire the onboard pipeline into village up (with detection) and add village onboard as a standalone command.

**File:** village/cli/lifecycle.py

**Changes to village up (existing command):**
- After runtime initialization, check needs_onboarding via detect_project()
- If needs_onboarding and not --skip-onboard:
  - Print 'Detected incomplete project setup. Starting onboarding...'
  - Run onboard pipeline (detector → scaffold → interview → generator → elder)
- If --skip-onboard:
  - Print warning about incomplete setup
- If not needs_onboarding:
  - Continue as normal (no changes)

**New command: village onboard:**
- Force-runs onboarding regardless of detection
- Takes --force flag to overwrite existing AGENTS.md/README.md
- Takes --skip-interview flag to use scaffold defaults without LLM interview
- Same pipeline: detector → scaffold → interview → generator → elder

**CLI options for village up:**
- --skip-onboard: Skip onboarding check, print warning
- --onboard: Force onboarding (alias behavior)

**CLI options for village new:**
- --skip-onboard: Use static templates instead of interview
- Default behavior: run full onboard pipeline

**Tests:** tests/test_cli_onboard.py
- Test village up detects incomplete project
- Test village up --skip-onboard prints warning

old_value: {"id":"bd-d8e","title":"Workflow YAML schema and loader","description":"Define WorkflowSchema dataclass with step definitions. Each step has: name, type (preset), prompt (inline) or policy (PPC file path), traits (dict), tools (list), target (string), input_from (optional - override implicit context), async (bool), retry config. Step type presets: prompt (base LLM call), critique (sequential_thinking + critical traits), decompose (sequential_thinking + target:beads), research (perplexity tool), synthesize (combine prior outputs), beads (create subtasks). Loader reads YAML from workflows/ dir. Files: village/workflow/__init__.py, village/workflow/schema.py, village/workflow/loader.py","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"github@bkuri.com","created_at":"2026-04-09T18:29:12Z","created_by":"Bernardo Kuri","updated_at":"2026-04-09T18:29:12Z"}

old_value: {"id":"bd-59g","title":"Add council types with debate meeting type","description":"Extend village council to support meeting types via --type flag (default: chat). Current chat behavior preserved as village council or village council -t chat. First new type: debate. Move village chat to be alias for village council. CLI: village council (show help), village council -t debate (start debate), village council -t debate -p skeptic -p pragmatist 'topic', village council list [--type debate], village council show \u003ccouncil-id\u003e, village council rematch \u003ccouncil-id\u003e. Missing topic triggers create-debate reverse-prompt workflow. Persona system: personas/*.md files with YAML frontmatter (name, model, temperature, tags + markdown body as system_prompt). village council -p \u003cname\u003e auto-creates persona if not found via create-persona reverse-prompt workflow. Debate engine: configurable rounds, wiki grounding via elder.ask(), resolution strategies (synthesis/vote/arbiter). Transcript archival to wiki/councils/\u003ccouncil-id\u003e/transcript.md, then elder pipeline. Missing critical params throw error in --json mode or trigger interview interactively. Files: village/council/ (new - engine.py, personas.py, resolution.py, transcript.py), village/cli/council.py (new), village/cli/chat.py (becomes thin alias), village/config.py (modified - council config), personas/ directory with examples.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"github@bkuri.com","created_at":"2026-04-09T04:56:06Z","created_by":"Bernardo Kuri","updated_at":"2026-04-09T04:56:06Z"}

old_value: {"id":"bd-7e0","title":"Add approval gates for village queue","description":"Add approval_threshold config in [queue] section of .village/config. Tasks at or below threshold enter PENDING_APPROVAL state before execution. CLI: village queue --approve \u003ctask-id\u003e, village queue --approve-all. Without approval, queue shows plan with pending items flagged. Non-interactive/CI mode errors on pending approvals. Modify state machine with PENDING_APPROVAL state and transitions.
